content: The 2024–2025 SOTA push for scalable active learning agent memory can be reframed as a recursive self-improvement problem, where the agent itself actively selects which fragments of its own memory architecture to retain, prune, and rewire for future planning, rather than just selecting new training data from an external dataset. This turns active learning from a data sampling strategy into an intrinsic, curiosity-driven mechanism for co-evolving the agent's memory structure and knowledge base.
